vulnerability.reason: Reference highlights resource inequality distorting robust rankings, whereas generated focuses on inability to meet cost assumptions without mention of ranking distortion; related but different sensitivity condition.
vulnerability.reference: Resource inequality in ability to enhance applications distorts robust rankings.
vulnerability.generated: Applicants' unequal resources to invest in costly essay optimization, leaving resource-constrained groups unable to meet the robust classifier's cost assumptions.

impact.reason: Generated's denial of admission to disadvantaged applicants and entrenchment of educational inequality semantically capture the reference's privileged applicants capturing elite slots and reduced diversity.
impact.reference: Privileged applicants capture elite slots, reducing class and racial diversity.
impact.generated: Socioeconomically disadvantaged applicants are denied admission, entrenching educational inequality.

dose_response.reason: The causal arc from minimax robust classification under cost uncertainty, through essay optimization and unequal resources, to exclusion and inequality is preserved in the generated text.
dose_response.reference: Minimax robust classification under cost uncertainty enables college applicants to optimize admissions essays and resumes, which in one-shot college admissions exposes resource inequality in ability to enhance applications, leading to privileged applicants capturing elite slots and reducing diversity.
dose_response.generated: Minimax robust strategic classification under cost uncertainty, through college applicants optimizing essays, triggers unequal resources to invest in costly optimization, leaving resource-constrained groups unable to meet cost assumptions, leading to socioeconomically disadvantaged applicants being denied admission, entrenching educational inequality.
